extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won the sprint on final stage after teammate Smith led out, celebrating before the line while Groves took stage 2 win of tour
tactical_patterns: VINE Jay exploited intermediate sprints (20km and <30km) to leapfrog GC position without attacking, targeting bonus seconds strategically.
SCULLY Tom pulled off early from leadout allowing GROVES to slot back through corners, then re-accelerate on sloping finish straight.
Team Bridgelane controlled peloton at 35-second gap for breakaway containing their points jersey rider, no urgency to close faster.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: mastered sloping finish gradient to win by margin allowing teammate to sit up and celebrate before line
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: showed tactical awareness targeting intermediate sprints rather than wasting energy in breaks to secure top-5 GC
rider_signals.HOFLAND Moreno: negative: had perfect leadout position but chose to drop back through corners and missed sprint opportunity entirely
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: negative: caught badly out of position in circuit finale finishing 50th despite being pre-race sprint favorite
rider_signals.HILL Ben: positive: attacked every stage to mathematically lock points jersey, understanding accumulation beats selective aggression
